summary: Proposes ITDA, which greedily builds dictionaries of raw activations via matching pursuit instead of training SAEs, cutting training cost to ~1% of SAE time and data. Reconstruction slightly lags SAEs, but dictionary Jaccard similarity beats CKA and SVCCA for cross-model comparison, scaling to Llama-3.1 405B.

Proves no existing preference-alignment loss is robust (redescending) under label noise and introduces Hölder-DPO, which provably is, yielding a gradient-free metric that localizes mislabeled feedback. Applied to Anthropic HH-RLHF, it reveals substantial noise whose removal improves alignment across methods.

Response in Trends in Cognitive Sciences defending the authors' hypothesis that prolonged infant immaturity supports foundation-model-style learning: acquiring rich, general-purpose representations from experience before goal-directed behavior emerges. Addresses critiques of the analogy between infant development and large-scale pretraining.

Book chapter weighing open frontier-model release: researcher access and transparency benefits versus cybersecurity and toxic-content risks. Argues today's open-weight models' benefits outweigh their risks, but future risk profiles are hard to forecast, recommending policy that encourages openness alongside transparency requirements and safety evaluations.

Handbook chapter introducing deep learning and reinforcement learning as tools for simulating language emergence, positioned against older agent-based, Bayesian, and genetic-algorithm approaches. Reviews key findings, limitations, and efforts toward more realistic simulations, targeting linguists and cognitive scientists studying language evolution.

SEAL jailbreaks large reasoning models by stacking multiple ciphers with randomly or adaptively varied length, order, and combination, overwhelming reasoning to bypass safety mechanisms and resist adaptive alignment. Achieves 80.8% attack success on GPT o4-mini, 27.2 points above prior baselines.

A multi-level graph neural surrogate with image-inspired spatial edge weights predicts high-fidelity sheet-metal forming outcomes in real time. On a real hot-stamping B-pillar case with varied blank shapes, it significantly outperforms three graph-based baselines at industrial-standard accuracy with low training and deployment cost.

Trains a 'Taboo' model that hints at a secret word never stated in training data or prompts, then tests elicitation: black-box strategies and largely automated mechanistic methods (logit lens, sparse autoencoders) both recover the secret, suggesting interpretability can surface hidden knowledge relevant to deception.

Builds LitmusValues, a pipeline measuring AI models' value prioritization from aggregate choices on AIRiskDilemmas, scenarios that pit values against each other in safety-relevant settings like power seeking. Measured value priorities, including innocuous ones like Care, predict risky behaviors both in-distribution and on unseen HarmBench tasks.

Chain-of-Modality prompts VLMs to reason sequentially over human demonstration videos paired with muscle-activity and audio signals, extracting task plans and control parameters like force that vision misses. Yields a threefold accuracy gain over baselines and transfers to new objects in real robot manipulation.

DemoStart, an auto-curriculum RL method, learns dexterous manipulation on a three-fingered robot hand from sparse rewards and a few simulated demonstrations, transferring zero-shot to the real robot from raw pixels and proprioception via domain randomization. Outperforms behavior cloning while needing 100x fewer demonstrations.

Shows LLMs can act as stochastic numerical optimizers for explainable robot policy search: the SAS (Summarize, Analyze, Synthesize) prompt iteratively retrieves, reasons over, and optimizes past robot traces to synthesize improved behavior, validated in simulation and on real-robot table tennis.

STEER trains robot manipulation policies on modular skills densely annotated in natural language, exposing an interface through which humans or VLMs orchestrate low-level control via commonsense reasoning. Learned skills compose into novel behaviors for unseen situations without additional data collection or retraining.

RT-Affordance conditions manipulation policies on affordances, robot poses at key task stages, as a lightweight intermediate representation between under-specified language and over-specified trajectories. A hierarchical model proposes an affordance plan from task language, leveraging web data and cheap affordance images to beat existing methods by over 50%.

Rottnest builds lightweight full-text and vector nearest-neighbor search indices on top of data lakes, extending columnar storage beyond relational analytics. Shown more cost-efficient than un-indexed lakes or specialized search databases across orders of magnitude of query loads and operating horizons.

System-card addendum for Codex, a cloud-based coding agent powered by codex-1, an o3 variant trained with reinforcement learning on real-world software engineering tasks to match human code style, follow instructions precisely, and iterate until tests pass. Documents the accompanying safety evaluations.

Argues representation superposition drives neural scaling laws, using Anthropic's toy model with weight decay to control superposition strength. Under strong superposition, loss scales inversely with model dimension regardless of feature-frequency distribution due to geometric overlap of representations, a regime consistent with open LLMs and Chinchilla scaling.

Survey of continual learning for LLMs organized along vertical continuity (general-to-specific adaptation via continual pre-training, domain-adaptive pre-training, and continual fine-tuning) and horizontal continuity (adaptation across time and domains), covering catastrophic forgetting, evaluation protocols, data sources, and open questions.

Improves differentially private heavy-hitter detection in the two-server model via a hash-prune-invert technique, narrowing the error-margin gap to the optimal central-model bound for identifying items above a frequency threshold while ignoring rare ones.

Evaluates LLMs for assisting human content moderators on hate speech, harassment, extremism, and election misinformation, reaching 90% agreement with human verdicts on 50,000 comments. Proposes five LLM-rater integration patterns; a real review-queue pilot improved rater-capacity use by 41.5% and precision/recall by 9-11 points.

Systematization-of-knowledge on watermarking AI-generated content: formalizes definitions, desired properties, objectives, and threat models; surveys representative schemes and evaluation strategies for attack robustness; and discusses open challenges, regulatory context, and watermarking's role against misinformation and deception.

HealthBench evaluates AI models on realistic healthcare scenarios using rubrics built with input from over 250 physicians, aiming to provide a shared standard for measuring model performance and safety in health contexts.

Formulates marketing budget allocation as an MDP over engagement and channel-performance signals and compares Q-learning, DQN, and PPO on historical campaign data. PPO performs best (7.4% CTR, 32.5% ROI, $9.20 CPA), with RL beating rule-based systems by 71% on conversions.

Benchmarks 27 frontier LLMs (Nov 2022-Apr 2025) on eight biology and biosecurity evaluations: top performance rose over 4x on the Virology Capabilities Test, with o3 doubling expert virologists, and several models matching experts on GPQA and WMDP biology. Chain-of-thought helped little; some benchmarks show saturation and data errors.

Establishes log-linear scaling laws for speculative-decoding draft acceptance rate along pretraining tokens, draft model capacity, and batch size. The resulting Scylla system coordinates scaling across these dimensions, beating EAGLE2 by 1.5-2.2x acceptance and doubling decoding throughput in industrial inference deployments.

Introduces a hierarchy of quantum optimization algorithms generalizing matrix multiplicative weight updates with an extra-gradient mechanism; the optimistic variant (OMMWU) computes epsilon-Nash equilibria of quantum zero-sum games quadratically faster than the 2008 Jain-Watrous algorithm's O(d/epsilon^2) convergence.

Survey of over 100 studies on 'slow thinking' reasoning LLMs like o1, organizing methods into test-time scaling (search, sampling, dynamic verification), reinforced learning (policy networks, reward models, self-evolution), and slow-thinking frameworks such as long chain of thought and hierarchical decomposition, with open challenges.

RLSQM uses reinforcement learning with a lightweight static-analysis reward model to steer LLM unit-test generation away from test smells, which appear in up to 37% of baseline outputs. Improves quality metrics by up to 23% with near-100% syntactic correctness, and a tuned Codex beats GPT-4 on all metrics.

Proves that linearizable asynchronous crash-tolerant read/write registers cannot serve purely local reads, then introduces almost-local reads (ALRs), a lightweight abstraction with near-single-node cost. ALR-enhanced ZAB and Hermes retain linearizability under asynchrony within 2-5% of original throughput.

Extends occupation-level LLM-exposure measures from 'GPTs are GPTs' to firms by combining them with Revelio Labs employee counts by occupation. Firms with more technology and AI-skilled workers show higher exposure, and between-category variation exceeds within-category variation, suggesting sizable productivity gains from LLM integration.
